input.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

source_payload.brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.

pinterest_cluster_labels: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

pins.title: Japandi Dining Room with Neutral Decor
pins.description: A Japandi dining room with neutral décor blends Japanese simplicity and Scandinavian warmth. Soft beige, warm wood, and gentle earth tones create a calm, inviting space. Clean lines, natural materials, and minimal furnishings keep the room feeling open and uncluttered, while subtle textures like linen, ceramic, and light wood add quiet elegance and comfort.

pins.title: Japandi Living Room: Calm Minimal Decor, Natural Wood & Indoor Plants
pins.description: Calm Japandi living room inspiration with a light neutral sofa, low wooden coffee table, soft textured rug, and plenty of indoor plants for a serene, balanced space. Minimal wall art, natural materials, and clean lines create an airy, soothing aesthetic—perfect for Japanese style interiors, minimalist living room ideas, and warm neutral home decor. Save for Japandi decor and plant-filled living room styling.
pins.cluster_label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table

cluster_summary.label: Japandi dining with rattan pendant
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Round oak dining table, woven rattan pendant, light linen-upholstered chairs, ceramic tableware. Stage with a single textured centerpiece (stoneware vase), neutral place settings, and warm-toned pendant to create an intimate focal point. Keep surfaces decluttered and emphasize wood grain and woven textures.

cluster_summary.label: Low-seating Japanese tea nook
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Floor-level cushions and low table on layered natural-fibre rug, bamboo/blind screens and soft lantern lighting. Arrange poufs and floor cushions for casual seating, add braided straw floor pads and low ambient lamps to enhance cozy, contemplative mood. Use warm, dimmable light and sparse greenery for balance.

cluster_summary.label: Neutral Japandi living with low coffee table
cluster_summary.visual_notes: Light neutral sofa on low wooden base, oversized low-profile coffee table, potted plants and minimal wall art. Stage with textured rugs (wool + tatami edge), mixed neutral cushions, sculptural ceramics and small bonsai or leafy plants to introduce organic color and scale. Emphasize horizontal lines and negative space.

vibe_report.summary: A calm, warm Japandi interior tuned for a young family: layered natural materials, low-profile furniture, and a designated cozy work corner. The palette leans warm-neutral with muted earth tones, plenty of horizontal lines and negative space, and carefully placed organic accents (plants, ceramics, woven textures). Scenes should feel uncluttered, tactile and lived-in without visual noise—soft sunlight, warm ambient lighting, and purposeful minimalism throughout.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: A warm-neutral base (#EDE7DF, #CBB698) with mid-tone woods (#A88461) and deeper grounding accents (#6D6A5F, #2B2724) provides family-friendly warmth while maintaining the serene, minimal Japandi look. Use desaturated greens via plants as the only saturated color to anchor life and scale.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Soft, warm ambient light with layered task and accent lighting. Day scenes emphasize soft sunlight through glazing and diffused window treatment; evening is warm and dimmable — rattan pendants and paper/linen shades for local pools of light.
materials: light oak and walnut woods (low-profile furniture frames)
woven rattan and seagrass (pendants, baskets, chair accents)
matte ceramic tableware and sculptural stoneware vases
natural fibre rugs (wool, jute, tatami-edge blends)
matte plaster or warm neutral paint for walls
textures: subtle wood grain, visible but not busy
low-pile wool and flatweave jute rugs
furniture_language: low, horizontal silhouettes (sofa, coffee table, bed)
rounded, simple forms (round dining table, sculptural vases)
slim, recessed legs on seating to create airy feel
multi-use pieces (bench that doubles as shoe storage)
integrated, inset shelving with minimal objects
styling_rules: keep surfaces intentionally sparse — 1–3 curated objects max
group ceramics/objects in odd numbers and low heights
layer a natural-fibre rug under low furniture to define zones
use plants selectively to add life and scale, not color
prioritize sightlines to the patio and natural light sources
avoid: bright, saturated colors or bold patterns
high gloss metal finishes (avoid reflective stainless appliances exposed in shots)
vertical visual clutter that competes with horizontal Japandi calm

room_guidance.headline: Living + Work Nook — layered low seating with a discreet standing desk
room_guidance.guidance: Center a low-profile sofa facing the patio; place a low wooden coffee table and textured rug to anchor the seating. Create a compact standing-desk nook near the glazing where daylight is strongest — choose a slim, oak-finish desk with a simple task lamp and cable management hidden. Add 2–3 medium plants to frame the entrance to the patio. Keep shelving minimal with 3–5 curated ceramics/books.
must_include: low-profile neutral sofa on wooden base
must_avoid: full-height office setups or large monitor arms

room_guidance.headline: Calm bedroom with family needs — safe crib + restful lines
room_guidance.guidance: Use a low platform bed with warm linen bedding and a small bedside pendant. Place the crib close to the bedroom window/wall to receive daylight but maintain required clearances; choose a simple, natural-wood crib. A small nursing/reading corner with a cozy chair and soft lamp is preferred over a large desk. Keep wall décor minimal — a single framed print or a woven wall piece.
must_include: low platform bed with warm linen
must_avoid: full-size standing desks or large office furniture in bedroom
excessive toys or bright nursery colors

room_guidance.headline: Intimate bay dining — round oak table and rattan pendant
room_guidance.guidance: Stage the bay dining nook with a round oak table sized for 4, linen-upholstered simple chairs, and a single woven rattan pendant centered above. A single stoneware vase with dried stems provides a quiet centerpiece. Keep table settings minimal — neutral ceramic plates, linen napkins.
must_avoid: too many place settings or clutter on table

warnings: Crib placement must respect clearance from open windows/glazing and nearby cords/lamp wires — visually stage with safe distances.
Standing desk should be compact and visible as a corner element; avoid staging an oversized office setup that breaks the family-living narrative.
Assumed daylight levels come from plan metadata; confirm glass door orientation in final pass to avoid mismatched shadows.

creative_spec.overall_mood: calm minimalism with earthy warmth and tactile layers — serene, functional, family-ready
global_style_notes: Keep all color accents desaturated and tied to natural materials; only plants introduce livelier greens.
Focus camera angles on horizontal lines and sightlines to the patio to sell the open-plan flow.
Texture is the primary source of visual interest: weave, linen, matte ceramics, and wood grain.
Avoid visible wires, busy appliance faces, or high-gloss reflections in camera-facing surfaces.
Scale furniture to each room's measured dimensions—favor smaller, low-profile pieces in the 11–13 ft spaces.
creative_spec.negative_prompt: no neon/saturated colors; no ornate or baroque elements; no heavy reflective stainless steel finishes; no cluttered surfaces; no visible people; no unrealistic floating furniture or incorrect scale proportions

caption_context.property_brief: Warm, calm home for a young family that needs a cozy work corner.
caption_context.aesthetic_summary: A calm, warm Japandi interior tuned for a young family: layered natural materials, low-profile furniture, and a designated cozy work corner. The palette leans warm-neutral with muted earth tones, plenty of horizontal lines and negative space, and carefully placed organic accents (plants, ceramics, woven textures). annotations.label: Mood
annotations.value: calm minimalism with earthy warmth and tactile layers — serene, functional, family-ready

warnings.message: Verify crib clearance from windows and any reachable cords; stage with safety-first placement.
Confirm the actual orientation of glazing relative to sun to avoid mismatched shadow direction in sequences.
Keep standing desk minimal to avoid visual mismatch with the Japandi aesthetic—large office setups will break the mood.
Crib placement must respect clearance from open windows/glazing and nearby cords/lamp wires — visually stage with safe distances.
Standing desk should be compact and visible as a corner element; avoid staging an oversized office setup that breaks the family-living narrative.
Assumed daylight levels come from plan metadata; confirm glass door orientation in final pass to avoid mismatched shadows.

evals.message: Strong match to the Nest living-room spec. All must-have items are present (low-profile oak sofa, low wooden coffee table, layered jute+wool rugs, standing desk by the glass door, 2 medium potted plants). Japandi styling, warm neutral palette, uncluttered surfaces, and realistic daylight + warm recessed fill are well executed. Minor nit: small visible cables under the desk could be tucked to fully meet the 'hidden cable' instruction, but this does not materially impact marketability.
Video is usable for the room sequence.

evals.message: Image is a strong, marketable Japandi bedroom: correct low platform bed, warm linen, natural-wood crib with safe clearance, small reading chair, warm bedside lighting, restrained palette and realistic scale. However the render is missing a required standing desk listed in the room spec—add a slim, compact standing desk (not a full-size office setup) in the work corner to meet the must‑include list. Otherwise lighting, staging, and architecture are excellent.

Strong Japandi bedroom: low platform bed, warm linens, natural-wood crib with apparent clearance, small reading chair, layered textures and soft bedside lighting are all well executed. The single hard fail is a missing required standing desk (the input spec repeatedly lists a standing desk as must_include). No banned elements (bright colors, heavy furniture, clutter, people) are present and lighting looks realistic. Recommend: add a compact standing desk or confirm whether the standing-desk requirement was an error; once the required desk is added (positioned to preserve the calm horizontal composition), resubmit for approval.
